target: data/sections.json — three "totality zone" → "totality footprint" prose updates (curmudgeon SEC-4.2.7-EXP272 c1 H2) and one angular-velocity qualifier symmetry fix (H4). All four are minor polish items, no numerics change.
decider_instructions: Four small unique find/replace patches in data/sections.json. Each find-string verified 1x match. No numbers change; no DATA blob, layer count, or classifier touched. Run tests/run.sh after applying (per CLAUDE.md tests-before-commit rule). Build will require regenerating docs/index.html via build.js publish. Optional H3 SVG-annotation-panel update (annotation panel only shows 0.9 sec rim-orbit upper bound, prose now also presents 3 sec mid-disc case) is OUT OF SCOPE — SVG assets live in docs/assets/ not data/sections.json; defer to social/integrity agent or follow-up patch.

text_patches.find: produce a totality zone roughly 1.3 km wide
text_patches.replace: produce a totality footprint roughly 1.3 km wide
text_patches.placement_rationale: 1x match. Static-framing residue — switching to "footprint" (instantaneous extent) aligns with the dynamic "track tracing the Moon's transit" framing already adopted elsewhere in §4.2.7. Preserves the 1.3 km figure and the 1/220 ratio that follows.

text_patches.find: force a totality zone roughly 1.3 km wide lasting
text_patches.replace: force a totality footprint roughly 1.3 km wide lasting
text_patches.placement_rationale: 1x match. Same rationale as P1. "Footprint lasting under one second" naturally describes the instantaneous shadow region at a given moment; the dwell-time at any single observer is what is under one second.
1x match. IMG alt-text appears in screen-reader output and image-search indexing; consistency with the prose framing is small-but-real.

1x match. Curmudgeon H4: rim-orbit phrase already qualified "at zenith" (~2°/min apparent shadow speed at zenith), but mid-disc phrase qualified "at h_Moon = 2,534 km" — both are zenith values since ω = v/h holds only when the moon is directly overhead. Symmetric "at zenith" qualifier closes a defender angle. One-clause delta, no math change.

math_verification.note: No numerical claims changed. All four patches are wording-only. The 1.3 km figure, 1/220 ratio, ~25 km/min mid-disc speed, ~0.5°/min angular velocity, 2,534 km Moon altitude, and 85 km penumbra width are all preserved verbatim.
math_verification.angular_velocity_check: ω = v/h is the small-angle approximation for the angular velocity of the shadow at the observer when the Moon is at zenith (h = vertical altitude). For v=25 km/min and h=2,534 km: ω = 25/2534 rad/min ≈ 0.00987 rad/min ≈ 0.565°/min ≈ ~0.5°/min ✓. This is only the at-zenith value; at lower elevation angles the apparent shadow speed differs. The curmudgeon's symmetric "at zenith" qualifier is correct.

not_addressed_this_run: SEC-4.2.7-EXP272.c1 H3 (SVG annotation panel only shows rim-orbit upper bound; prose now also shows 3 sec mid-disc case). Defer: SVG asset docs/assets/eclipse-geometry-globe-vs-dome.svg requires a separate SVG-edit patch outside sections.json. Recommend: route to social or integrity agent, or open a follow-up issue.

SEC-4.2.7-EXP272.c1 H1 (orbit-the-moon-even-slower escape closure). Curmudgeon rated as moderate but flagged as already-robust; defender would still face the ~11 km/min lower-bound from observed Moon angular motion (~0.25°/min). Worth a follow-up but not in this small-polish singleton.

anticipated_objections.objection: "totality footprint" is non-standard astrophysics terminology; "umbral footprint" or "shadow footprint" is more conventional.
anticipated_objections.response: "Totality footprint" is unambiguous in this context — the prose has already established (multiple times) that the dome geometry produces totality with 1.3 km extent, so reading "totality footprint" as "footprint of the totality region" is the natural parse. "Umbral footprint" would technically be the umbra (full shadow) ground-projection, which is fine but introduces "umbra" terminology not previously used in this prose. Keep "totality footprint" for consistency with the section's existing vocabulary.

anticipated_objections.objection: The H4 "at zenith" qualifier is technically redundant with the small-angle approximation already implicit in ω = v/h. Why bother?
anticipated_objections.response: Symmetry with the rim-orbit phrase "~2°/min apparent shadow speed at zenith" — a defender comparing the two halves of the parenthetical would notice the asymmetric qualifier and use it as an angle of attack ("why was zenith specified there but not here?"). One-clause fix preempts the angle.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's geometry-imported eclipse predictions are quantitatively wrong by orders of magnitude (1.3 km footprint vs 294 km observed; ~1 second dwell vs ~2 min 18 s observed); the polish patches do not change that conclusion, only tighten the language so a careful skeptical reader sees no static-vs-dynamic confusion and no asymmetric qualifiers to nitpick on.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is internal prose polish, not a new dome-claim engagement. The kill remains as documented in §4.2.7 (1:220 width ratio, sub-second dwell time).
other_side_of_the_argument: A defender might note that "zone" is a more reader-friendly word than "footprint" — flat-earth audiences may parse "zone" more readily. Counter: the prose elsewhere uses "track" and "swath" and "path" interchangeably for the time-integrated trajectory; reserving "footprint" for the instantaneous extent and the other terms for the trajectory clarifies the static-vs-dynamic distinction the curmudgeon flagged. The substantive argument is robust under either vocabulary; the polish is for consistency.
cross_references: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-272.json (the EXP that originally added the prose being polished)
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json ISS-1743 (the open issue this closes)

25 km/min ÷ 2534 km = 0.00987 rad/min × (180/π) = 0.565°/min ≈ ~0.5°/min ✓ at zenith only

notes_for_decider: Singleton scope; expected commit-message form: "Decider <ts>: EXP-411 SEC-4.2.7 polish — 3 totality-zone→footprint substitutions + 1 angular-velocity zenith-symmetry fix (ISS-1743)". After integration, decider should also close ISS-1743 (or mark fixed-pending-verification for SVG H3 follow-up, depending on whether you treat H3 as a separate open issue).
